# ArgoCD Helm Ops — Observability Stack

This skill guides you through deploying, syncing, and debugging the Helm charts
that make up the OpenShift observability stack managed by ArgoCD ApplicationSet.

## Stack Overview

All apps are generated by `bootstrap/argocd/applicationset-observability.yaml`.
Charts live under `bootstrap/helm/<name>/`.

| App | Chart | Namespace |
|---|---|---|
| coo-monitoring-stack | bootstrap/helm/coo-monitoring-stack | openshift-monitoring / coo |
| tempo-stack | bootstrap/helm/tempo-stack | openshift-tempo-operator |
| opentelemetry-stack | bootstrap/helm/opentelemetry-stack | openshift-opentelemetry-operator |
| observability-servicemonitors | bootstrap/helm/observability-servicemonitors | various |
| logging-operator | bootstrap/helm/logging-operator | openshift-logging |
| network-observability-stack | bootstrap/helm/network-observability-stack | netobserv |
| perses | bootstrap/helm/perses | perses |

## Step 1 – Validate Chart Locally

Before pushing any Helm change, always render and validate:

```bash
cd bootstrap/helm/<chart-name>
helm template . --values values.yaml | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f - 2>&1 | head -40
```

If you need to test with specific overrides:
```bash
helm template . --values values.yaml --set key=value | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f -
```

## Step 2 – Check ArgoCD App Status

```bash
# List all observability apps
oc get applications -n openshift-gitops | grep -E 'NAME|observ|tempo|coo|otel|perses|logging|netobserv|showroom'

# Detailed status of a specific app
oc get application <app-name> -n openshift-gitops -o yaml | \
  python3 -c "import sys,yaml,json; d=yaml.safe_load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d.get('status',{}), indent=2))" 2>/dev/null | \
  grep -A5 '"conditions"\|"health"\|"sync"' | head -40
```

## Step 3 – Force Refresh / Sync

```bash
# Refresh (re-evaluate, no sync)
oc annotate application <app-name> -n openshift-gitops \
  argocd.argoproj.io/refresh=normal --overwrite

# Hard refresh (clear cache)
oc annotate application <app-name> -n openshift-gitops \
  argocd.argoproj.io/refresh=hard --overwrite

# Force sync via ArgoCD CLI (if available)
argocd app sync <app-name> --force
```

## Step 4 – Diagnose Operator Install Failures

### Check Subscription / CSV
```bash
OPERATOR_NS=openshift-operators   # all operators in this stack use AllNamespaces

oc get subscription -n $OPERATOR_NS
oc get csv -n $OPERATOR_NS | grep -E 'NAME|tempo|coo|opentelemetry|loki|netobserv'

# Stuck InstallPlan?
oc get installplan -n $OPERATOR_NS
oc describe installplan <name> -n $OPERATOR_NS | grep -A10 'Status\|Conditions'
```

### Reset a stuck operator
```bash
oc delete subscription <name> -n openshift-operators
# ArgoCD will recreate it automatically on next sync
```

### Get operator pod logs
```bash
# COO
oc logs -n openshift-operators deployment/cluster-observability-operator

# Tempo
oc logs -n openshift-operators deployment/tempo-operator-controller

# OpenTelemetry
oc logs -n openshift-operators deployment/opentelemetry-operator-controller-manager
```

## Step 5 – Diagnose CRD Reconciliation (TempoStack / MonitoringStack)

### Check CR status
```bash
# TempoStack
oc describe tempostack tempo -n openshift-tempo-operator | grep -A20 'Conditions:'

# MonitoringStack
oc describe monitoringstack <name> -n <namespace> | grep -A20 'Conditions:'
```

### Common errors and fixes

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `unknown field "spec.prometheusConfig.resources"` | Old schema (pre v1.3.1) | Move `resources:` to top-level `spec.resources:` |
| `Secret "tempo-s3" not found` | OBC secret mismatch | Recreate secret with correct field names (see storage section) |
| `tls: failed to verify certificate` | NooBaa port 443 requires TLS | Use HTTP endpoint on port 80 |
| `OperatorGroup already exists` | Duplicate OG in openshift-operators | Remove OG creation for AllNamespaces operators |

### COO schema — v1.3.1 CORRECT format
```yaml
# WRONG (v1.2.x):
spec:
  prometheusConfig:
    resources:
      limits: { memory: 512Mi }

# CORRECT (v1.3.1+):
spec:
  resources:
    limits: { memory: 512Mi }
```

## Step 6 – ODF / OBC Storage Troubleshooting

When `storage.type: odf`, the chart creates an ObjectBucketClaim. The OBC produces:
- **Secret** `<bucketName>`: fields `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`
- **ConfigMap** `<bucketName>`: fields `BUCKET_NAME`, `BUCKET_HOST`, `BUCKET_PORT`

Tempo expects different field names (`access_key_id`, `access_key_secret`, `bucket`, `endpoint`).
A manual secret bridging OBC → Tempo format must be created.

```bash
# Get OBC-generated values
OBC_NAME=tempo-s3
NS=openshift-tempo-operator

AK=$(oc get secret $OBC_NAME -n $NS -o jsonpath='{.data.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}' | base64 -d)
SK=$(oc get secret $OBC_NAME -n $NS -o jsonpath='{.data.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}' | base64 -d)
BUCKET=$(oc get configmap $OBC_NAME -n $NS -o jsonpath='{.data.BUCKET_NAME}')
HOST=$(oc get configmap $OBC_NAME -n $NS -o jsonpath='{.data.BUCKET_HOST}')
PORT=$(oc get configmap $OBC_NAME -n $NS -o jsonpath='{.data.BUCKET_PORT}')

# Use HTTP (port 80) to avoid TLS issues with self-signed NooBaa certs
oc create secret generic tempo-s3-formatted -n $NS \
  --from-literal=access_key_id="$AK" \
  --from-literal=access_key_secret="$SK" \
  --from-literal=bucket="$BUCKET" \
  --from-literal=endpoint="http://${HOST}:80"
```

## Step 7 – Sync Wave Reference

All resources need `argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave` annotations:

```yaml
# Wave 0: Namespaces
# Wave 1: Operators (Subscription, OperatorGroup only if not openshift-operators)
# Wave 2: RBAC, Secrets, ObjectBucketClaims
# Wave 3: CRs (MonitoringStack, TempoStack, OpenTelemetryCollector)
# Wave 4: UI Plugins (optional)
```

Missing wave annotations = CRs deploy before their CRDs exist → sync failure with `no matches for kind`.

Always add to every resource:
```yaml
metadata:
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "3"
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-options: SkipDryRunOnMissingResource=true
```

## Quick Diagnostic Sequence

Run this when an app is OutOfSync or Degraded:

```bash
APP=tempo-stack
NS=openshift-gitops

# 1. See sync error
oc get application $APP -n $NS -o jsonpath='{.status.operationState.message}' && echo

# 2. See health/sync conditions
oc get application $APP -n $NS \
  -o jsonpath='{range .status.conditions[*]}{.type}: {.message}{"\n"}{end}'

# 3. See which resources are out of sync
oc get application $APP -n $NS \
  -o jsonpath='{range .status.resources[*]}{.kind}/{.name}: {.status} {.health.status}{"\n"}{end}' | \
  grep -v 'Synced Healthy'
```
